mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales as measured by RSAFS indicate expanding consumer spending, which boosts corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If RSAFS month-over-month growth is positive but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 60% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals improving labor market conditions, which raises consumer confidence and spending, leading to positive equity returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points from its value three months prior
failure_condition: If in triggered periods the median 20-day S&P 500 return is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low federal funds rates combined with accelerating housing starts create a powerful positive feedback loop: low rates stimulate housing construction, which boosts economic activity and equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with housing recovery
failure_condition: If the joint signal (FEDFUNDS < 1% and HOUST > 700k) produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of instances, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-crisis recovery, strong industrial production growth (INDPRO) reflects expanding manufacturing output, which leads to higher corporate earnings and supports positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive but 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are negative in more than 50% of months during the post-crisis period, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating inflation as measured by CPIAUCSL triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: If in periods with CPIAUCSL YoY > 2% the 20-day S&P 500 return is positive more often than negative, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) and retail sales (RSAFS) signals robust consumer-led economic expansion, which drives corporate profits and lifts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the joint signal (both PAYEMS and RSAFS month-over-month growth above 0.5%) does not result in positive 60-day returns in at least 70% of occurrences, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals improving economic activity and higher capacity utilization, which leads to immediate positive sentiment and short-term equity gains.
failure_condition: If INDPRO month-over-month growth is positive but the 5-day S&P 500 return is negative in more than 55% of observations, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above a key threshold signals a self-sustaining recovery in residential investment, which has significant multiplier effects on the economy and boosts equity prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If in months when HOUST > 700k the 60-day S&P 500 return is negative more than 40% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.
